steps.title: Identify the implicit frequency contrast
steps.content: The passage states routes are “rarely designed to simply reach a summit,” then immediately describes what they focus on instead. The transition word needs to bridge what these routes rarely do versus what they more often do.

steps.title: Apply the frequency shift
steps.content: ”More often” directly expresses the shift: routes rarely focus on summits, but more often on recreation and scenery. The other three mischaracterize this relationship—treating recreation as supplementary (Additionally), creating a false contrast (On the other hand), or implying unexpected conflict (Nonetheless) instead of natural consequence. This matches choice C — C is correct.
